planets.name: Ignis
planets.desc: A scorched world locked in a slow rotation. Rivers of molten silicate carve luminous veins across its darkened crust. Tidal forces from the parent star keep the interior in a state of perpetual convection.

planets.name: Terra Nova
planets.desc: A vibrant terrestrial world with vast emerald continents and deep blue oceans. A thick nitrogen-oxygen atmosphere supports complex weather systems and a thriving biosphere.

planets.name: Jupiterus
planets.desc: A massive gas giant with dramatic banded cloud patterns driven by 600 km/h winds. Its Great Storm — a colossal anticyclone — has persisted for centuries in the southern hemisphere.

planets.name: Glacius
planets.desc: A frozen world encased in kilometres of crystalline ice. Beneath the surface, a subsurface ocean warmed by tidal heating may harbour primitive life in hydrothermal vent systems.

planets.name: Nereid
planets.desc: A majestic ice giant with vivid azure bands of methane clouds. Extreme axial tilt creates dramatic seasonal shifts, and a faint ring system of dark icy particles encircles the equator.

planets.name: Arrakis
planets.desc: A vast desert planet swept by titanic dust storms that can engulf the entire globe. Ancient riverbeds hint at a watery past, and polar caps of frozen CO₂ expand and retreat with the seasons.

planets.name: Oceanus
planets.desc: A world entirely covered by a deep global ocean with no solid land. Towering wave systems driven by three moons create complex tidal patterns. Bioluminescent organisms light the twilight depths.

planets.name: Venomia
planets.desc: A hellish Venus-analog shrouded in impenetrable layers of sulfuric acid clouds. Runaway greenhouse heating maintains surface temperatures hot enough to melt lead. Volcanic lightning crackles through the dense yellow-green atmosphere, and super-rotating winds circle the planet in just four days.

planets.name: Carbonis
planets.desc: An exotic world built from carbon rather than silicates. Vast plains of dark graphite stretch between mountain ranges of crystalline diamond. The thin hydrocarbon atmosphere refracts starlight into prismatic halos, and the surface glitters with sharp faceted reflections under any illumination.

planets.name: Ferria
planets.desc: A hyper-dense iron world with a surface of polished metal plains and rust-red oxide canyons. Its powerful magnetic field traps stellar wind into shimmering auroral curtains. Ancient tectonic stresses have exposed veins of pure nickel-iron that gleam like mirrors under starlight.

planets.name: Luminara
planets.desc: A world of towering crystalline spires and vast faceted plains formed under extreme pressure. Silicate and quartz megastructures refract light into dazzling prismatic rainbows. The thin atmosphere scatters short wavelengths, bathing the surface in an ethereal blue-violet glow.
